name: React Counter (Vite + TS)
description: Build a React counter component with Vitest tests using Vite + TypeScript.
prompt: Build a React counter application with Vite and TypeScript. 1. Scaffold the project: npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts npm install npm install -D @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event jsdom 2. Configure Vitest in vite.config.ts: Add test: { globals: true, environment: 'jsdom', setupFiles: ['./src/setupTests.ts'] } to the defineConfig 3. Create src/setupTests.ts with: import '@testing-library/jest-dom'; 4. Create src/Counter.tsx with a Counter component that: - Has a numeric count state (starts at 0) - Shows the count in a {count} - Has an Increment button that adds 1 to count (data-testid="increment") - Has a Decrement button that subtracts 1 (data-testid="decrement") - Has a Reset button that sets count back to 0 (data-testid="reset") 5. Create src/Counter.test.tsx that tests: - Initial count is 0 - Increment button increases count by 1 - Decrement button decreases count by 1 - Reset button resets count to 0 6. Add to package.json scripts: "test": "vitest run" 7. Run and confirm tests pass: npm test Do not use stubs. Implement real state management with useState.
